rules.statement: Every Class A passive_gate mapping entry (attestation_class: 'A') MUST carry an enforced_by param whose dotted Python path resolves to an existing class or function in src/. Stale enforced_by claims (referencing renamed, moved, or deleted symbols) are a blocking violation: they make the governance claim silently false while the engine still returns ok=True.
rules.rationale: ADR-142. passive_gate is the semantically correct engine for rules whose enforcement lives in runtime substrate. That correctness depends on the enforced_by claim being true. A stale claim — symbol deleted, renamed, or moved — turns a legitimate governance delegation into silent governance absence: every audit passes ok=True on a claim that no longer holds. Initial recon (2026-07-05) found one already-stale reference (features.maintenance.command_sync_service in .intent/enforcement/mappings/infrastructure/cli_commands.yaml; actual path is body.maintenance.command_sync_service). The check is purely AST/filesystem: it parses enforced_by as a dotted path, resolves the longest matching prefix to a source file, and confirms the named symbol is defined in that file. No imports, no runtime execution.
